name: Dochen, Sanford
resolved_employer: IBM (retired 2019) — Corporate Citizenship / Corporate Social Responsibility Manager for Texas, Arkansas and Louisiana; now board director, Amplify Credit Union
evidence: Sanford "Sandy" Dochen is a native Austinite who spent ~21 years at IBM as its regional corporate citizenship/CSR manager (retired 2019), is past chair of the Austin Area Research Organization, and now sits on the boards of Amplify Credit Union and the Anti-Defamation League. No zip on the county record, so identity rests on the distinctive surname plus a well-documented Austin civic profile consistent with a small donation to a Travis County commissioner; "Sandy" is the standard diminutive of Sanford. Note: this is NOT the Dochen Realtors / Dochen Realty Group brokerage — no public source ties him to that firm, and no real-estate connection was assumed.

affiliations.org: Anti-Defamation League (and Jewish Federation of Austin)
affiliations.role: Board member (ADL); registered witness FOR SB 326 at Texas House Education K-16 hearing, 3/25/2025, representing "Self; Anti defamation League, Jewish Federation of Austin"
affiliations.snippet: Witness list entry: "Sandy Dochen (Self; Anti defamation League, Jewish Federation of Austin)" — registering, but not testifying, FOR SB 326. Recorded as jewish_civic because this is direct state legislative advocacy, not private communal membership.
Listed as a Director on the board of Amplify Credit Union, an Austin-headquartered financial institution.

affiliations.role: Past chair; also Leadership Austin fellow, Science Mill board
affiliations.snippet: Bio: retired from IBM in 2019 from a regional corporate social responsibility position; "a consummate community volunteer"; active in the Austin Area Research Organization and a Leadership Austin fellow.

name: Sawin, Kodi
resolved_employer: Sawin Group — Principal / Public Affairs Strategist (infrastructure, water and land-use policy consulting)
evidence: Kodi Elizabeth Sawin is principal of Sawin Group, an Austin public-affairs/government-relations consultancy focused on water, land use and large infrastructure permitting; the firm's listed address is PO Box 12104, Austin TX 78711, an exact match to the donor zip. She is also a Travis County Citizens Bond Advisory Committee appointee for Precinct 3 — the precinct represented by Ann Howard, the recipient of this donation — which independently corroborates the identity. She ran as an Independent for Texas House District 19 (endorsed by the Texas Forward Party). Not found in Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registrations, though her work is lobbying-adjacent public-affairs consulting.

affiliations.snippet: County bio: "Kodi Sawin specializes in analyzing politically complex infrastructure and natural resource projects" — assesses political risk and executes public strategy for projects requiring policy change at local, regional and state levels.

affiliations.org: Travis County Citizens Bond Advisory Committee
affiliations.snippet: Listed on the Travis County website as a Precinct 3 appointee to the Citizens Bond Advisory Committee.

affiliations.org: Texas Forward Party / Kodi Sawin for Texas (HD19)
affiliations.role: Independent candidate for Texas House District 19; party endorsee
affiliations.snippet: The Texas Forward Party announced its endorsement of Kodi Sawin for Texas House of Representatives, citing her commitment to moving Texas past partisan gridlock.

name: Mennucci, William
resolved_employer: Valli Kane & Vagnini LLP, Austin (previously Thompson, Coe, Cousins & Irons LLP) — trial and appellate attorney
evidence: William Leonard "Bill" Mennucci is the only William Mennucci with a public Austin footprint: State Bar of Texas #00788042, licensed 1993 (UT J.D.), board certified in Civil Appellate Law, practicing personal-injury litigation and civil appeals at Valli Kane & Vagnini LLP in Austin after a long run at defense firm Thompson Coe. Donor zip 78751 is a central-Austin residential zip consistent with an Austin-based attorney, but the residential zip itself is not independently confirmed against him, hence medium rather than high. No TEC lobbyist registration found; no federal PAC giving found under his name (an Anita Mennucci of Austin 78723 appears in FEC records, likely a relative, not this donor).

name: Lawrence, Stanton
evidence: Could not confirm identity. The only Texan named Stanton Lawrence with a public profile is Dr. Stanton Eugene Lawrence, a career educator (teacher, principal, assistant superintendent and superintendent in Texas and Missouri) with M.Ed. and Ed.D. degrees from UT Austin, now regional coordinator for Huntsville/East Texas at the prison-reentry nonprofit Bridges To Life; he is publicly listed as residing in Cleveland, TX (Huntsville area), well outside Travis County. The county record carries no zip, so nothing corroborates him as the donor to Jeff Travillion beyond the name and a UT Austin educational tie. All three mandatory searches were run and returned nothing: no TEC lobbyist registration under "Lawrence, Stanton" (the registry lists only unrelated Lawrences, e.g. "Lawrence, Kevin F."), and no identifiable federal PAC giving.

name: Braunagel-Brown, Mary
resolved_employer: State University of New York (SUNY) system, 20-year career (retired 2003); philanthropist/UT Austin donor
evidence: Mary Braunagel-Brown (B.A. '68, M.A., Ph.D. '83, UT Austin) worked ~20 years for the SUNY system in upstate New York and retired to Austin with her husband Bruce Brown in 2003. Extremely distinctive hyphenated name with only one public bearer in Austin; her large volume of small-dollar Dem-leaning federal giving is consistent with a retired academic administrator. She is an Osage Nation member and funded the first-ever endowment for UT's Native American and Indigenous Studies program, using income she receives as an Osage shareholder from tribal oil rights — a passive inherited headright interest, not an industry role, so it is recorded here as context only and NOT flagged as an oil_gas affiliation.

affiliations.org: University of Texas at Austin — Native American and Indigenous Studies (NAIS) Program
affiliations.role: Founding endowment donor; namesake of the Mary Braunagel-Brown Excellence Fund and the Braunagel-Brown Endowed Lecture. Also gave the initial gift to the INSPIRE Leadership program at UT's Center for Women and Gender Studies (2013).
affiliations.snippet: UT's NAIS program hosts "The Braunagel-Brown Endowed Lecture in Native American & Indigenous Studies," launched fall 2023 from her endowment gift.

name: Hale, Richard
evidence: Could not identify despite targeted digging into the flagged anomaly (255 FEC donations at a ~0.005 lean, i.e. near-uniformly Republican federal giving, alongside a 2017 gift to Democratic county commissioner Jeff Travillion). No public footprint was found for a Richard Hale in Manor TX 78653: no LinkedIn, business registration, news coverage, obituary, or bio page. Same-named individuals found are elsewhere in Texas and not corroborable (a Houston obituary; Richard W. Hale, a Decatur TX police officer who died in 2018). Specifically checked for oil/gas, gun-rights, and military-defense ties and found nothing attributable to this person. Not a registered Texas lobbyist — the TEC lists contain no Richard Hale (nearest: Hale Justin D., Hale Angela). FEC individual-contribution occupation/employer records for Manor TX 78653 could not be retrieved (FEC API HTTP 429 rate-limit; OpenSecrets 403), so the partisan-lean pattern could not be attached to a verified identity or employer. The high donation count with a uniform partisan lean is consistent with recurring small-dollar online giving (e.g. WinRed-style), but that is an inference, not a sourced finding. No affiliation recorded rather than guessed. RECOMMEND: re-run the FEC Schedule A lookup for contributor_zip=78653 once API quota resets to recover employer/occupation.
